name: VISTEC ENCEBOLLADO
intro: This is my vegan take on bistec encebollado, which translates to “steak and onions.” Throughout the time I attended high school, my Tio Ramon lived with us. He would cook the most succulent food and I remember coming home to the aroma of garlic, herbs and onions in the air. My mouth is watering now just thinking about it. One day specifically, he was making the juiciest steak and onions I’ve ever had in my life. You know I had to put a vegan spin on it, and using Beyond Meat was the perfect substitute. It is easy to manipulate to create the same beeflike texture and taste, and it’s gluten- and soy-free. If this choice of vegan meat isn’t your thing, try using portobello mushrooms instead. Marinating the meat is important. Take your time making this dish; you want the flavors to blend very well. Let’s get into it.
method: Season the frozen patties with 2 tablespoons (30 ml) of the olive oil, the adobo and 1 tablespoon (4 g) of the oregano. Marinate the patties in the refrigerator in plastic wrap or a ziplock bag until they thaw. Once the patties are thawed, you can shape each into a rectangle to look like cubed steak.
In a large pan over medium heat, heat 2 tablespoons (30 ml) of the olive oil and add the yellow and red onions. Cook until the onions soften a bit.

Transfer the onions to a bowl and set aside. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons (30 ml) of olive oil to the pan, plus the sofrito, garlic and vegetable bouillon cube, breaking down the cube. Add both patties and cook them on both sides for 4 minutes.
Add your vegetable stock, tomato sauce, red wine vinegar, bay leaves and the onions. Mix and let everything simmer for 7 minutes. This should allow all the flavors to settle into the onions and meat.
Serve with potatoes or my Quinoa con Gandules.
